Psychographic-Microeconomic Engine V6.1
SEM → CBC → Market Structure → φ-Pricing → MR=MC · 5-step chained analysis
🌍 No country V6.1 φ-Chain
SEM
→
CBC
→
P4 Structure
→
φ-Pricing
→
MR=MC
① Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) — Psychological Weighting
Calculates latent psychological weighting factors that shift the demand curve in Step 2. Scores on a 1–7 Likert scale.
Perceived Value (1–7)
Brand Trust (1–7)
Cognitive Friction — lower=better (1–7)
Social Proof (1–7)
⚡ Data injected from SEM — Utility Multiplier applied to base part-worths
② Choice-Based Conjoint (CBC) — Market Share & Price Elasticity
Simulates market share using Multinomial Logit and extracts arc price elasticity of demand. SEM utility multiplier adjusts base part-worths.
Option A Price
Option B Price (Competitor)
⚡ Data injected from CBC — Price Elasticity imported from demand simulation
③ Market Structure & Pricing Power (Lerner Index + HHI)
Maps HHI market concentration and CBC elasticity to optimal markup using the Lerner Index formula: P* = MC / (1 + 1/e).
HHI Score (0–10,000) — 3200
Competitive (0)Oligopoly (2500)Monopoly (10k)
Base Marginal Cost (MC)
⚡ Data injected from P4 — Optimal Price (P*) set as Golden Ratio base
④ Fibonacci & Golden Ratio (φ) Pricing Architecture
Applies φ = 1.618 to build three pricing tiers from the validated P*. Also generates Fibonacci production batch sequence with marginal cost scaling.
Base Tier Price (from P4 P*)
Base Batch (units)
Base MC per unit
⚡ Data injected from φ — Final Price Tiers and MC parameters applied
⑤ Final Marginal Analysis — Profit Maximisation (MR = MC)
Calculates profit-maximising output quantity Q* where MR = MC, using all chained parameters.
Validated Price (from φ Tier 3)
Fixed Costs
Variable Cost / Unit (from φ MC)
Diseconomy Factor (k)

🔁 Delta Write-Back Store v10 NEW
Recalibration directives are automatically extracted from every ARTEMIS run and stored here. Each module reads these on its next run and injects the adjustments as prompt priors — closing the autonomous learning loop.

🧠 Phase 1 — Self-Learning (RunMemory)
ACTIVE
Every module run is stored with its input context, output quality, engine, and country. Before each run, the top relevant past runs are automatically injected as few-shot examples — making every module smarter with each use. Memory is portable via USB export/import.
Run Memory Log
No runs stored yet. Run any module to begin learning.
🔬 Phase 2 — Self-Improvement (PromptEvolution)
Analyzes all module runs with quality scores below the threshold. Sends the original prompt + output + score to the AI to generate an improved prompt. The evolved prompt replaces the original and is versioned for rollback. Requires minimum 3 runs per module.
Quality Threshold (evolve below)
Target Module (blank = all)
Evolving prompts…
No prompt evolutions yet.
⚗ Phase 3 — Self-Development (DevAgent)
SANDBOX ISOLATED
Describe a capability gap and DevAgent generates a complete new module — HTML panel, JS logic, system prompt, and nav registration — then injects it live into ApexIQ. All generated modules run in a sandboxed context and require your explicit approval before activation.
Capability Gap Description
Module Category

💾 SanDisk Storage Hub
Connect your USB drive · Auto-save · Download & Upload files
● Not Connected
① How to Connect Your SanDisk Drive
1
Plug in your SanDisk USB drive to your device. On Android, use the USB-C OTG adapter that came with your SanDisk drive. Wait for the drive icon to appear in your notification bar.
2
Tap the button below — "Connect SanDisk Drive". Your browser will open a folder picker dialog.
3
In the folder picker, navigate to your SanDisk drive — it appears as a storage location on the left side. Tap to open it.
4
You do not need to open any sub-folder. Tap "Use this folder" or "Allow" at the bottom of the screen. ApexIQ will create its own _SYNC folder automatically.
5
When asked "Allow Chrome to access files on this storage device?" — tap Allow. This is required for ApexIQ to write files directly to your drive without asking every time.
ⓘ Note: The connection permission resets every time you reload the app. You only need to repeat steps 2–5 — the folder location is remembered. This is a browser security requirement, not an ApexIQ limitation.
✓ Drive Connected
Drive folder
Last saved
Auto-saveActive ● After every module run
Save file_SYNC/latest-full-sync.json
② Save Data to Drive (Download Path)
🌟 Auto-Save (Recommended — requires drive connection)
When your SanDisk drive is connected, ApexIQ saves automatically to _SYNC/latest-full-sync.json after every module run. Nothing to do — it just happens.
Connect your drive above to enable auto-save.
📦 Save Everything Now (Full Sync)
Saves all module outputs, session data, AI memory, integrity log, and API keys to a single file. Includes API keys — keep this file private.
What is saved: All module outputs · Country Intelligence profile · AI memory (RunMemory) · Integrity log · API keys (Full Sync only) · ARTEMIS calibration history · SynthoSurvey runs · Co-Pilot session memory
③ Restore from Drive (Upload Path)
To restore a previous session, import the sync file from your SanDisk drive. You do not need the drive to be connected — you can also pick the file from your Downloads folder.
1
On your drive, the sync file is at _SYNC/latest-full-sync.json. If you used manual save, it is in your Downloads folder named apexiq-sync-[date].json.
2
Tap "Choose File to Import" below. Navigate to the file on your drive or Downloads and tap it.
3
ApexIQ will restore all module outputs, your country profile, AI memory, and the integrity log. API keys are restored only from Full Sync files.
ⓘ Import is additive, not destructive. Importing merges data with your current session — it does not erase what you already have. If the same run exists in both, the existing one is kept.
❗ Troubleshooting
Drive not appearing in folder picker
Make sure the SanDisk drive is plugged in and the USB-C OTG adapter is firmly seated. On Android, pull down the notification shade — the drive should appear as a storage device. If not, unplug and replug the drive.
Save goes to Downloads instead of the drive
This happens when the drive connection permission has expired (browser security resets permissions on reload). Tap "Reconnect" at the top of this page and select your drive folder again. Auto-save will then write directly to the drive.
File picker says "Access denied" or drive is greyed out
Some Android versions restrict external storage access via the browser. Solution: use the manual save option (Full Sync button) which downloads to your Downloads folder, then manually move the file to your SanDisk drive using your file manager app.
My browser does not support drive connection
Direct drive connection requires Chrome 86+ or Edge 86+. Safari and Firefox do not support it. If you are on Safari, use the manual Full Sync export and import workflow instead — it works in all browsers.
Getting Started
Core Intelligence
Proprietary Engines

Operations
Research & New Modules
⚙ SWARM War-Gaming
v20 Features
Reference
⧡ Quick Start Guide
ApexIQ v20 is a self-contained global AI business intelligence platform running entirely in a single HTML file — no server, no installation, no external dependencies beyond the AI engine of your choice.
What You Need
One free API key is all you need to activate the full platform. Get one from any of these sources at zero cost:
• Google Gemini 2.5 Flash — aistudio.google.com (free tier, generous limits)
• Groq — console.groq.com (free, very fast)
• GitHub Models — github.com/marketplace/models (free with GitHub account)
First 5 Minutes
1
Open Settings
Click Settings & Engines at the bottom of the left sidebar. Find your engine (e.g. Google Gemini), paste your API key, and click 💾 Save Settings.
2
Set Session Context
Click 📌 Set Context on the Command Bridge. Enter your company name, industry, and target market. This context is automatically injected into every module prompt — once per session is all you need.
3
Run Your First Module
Navigate to Market Intelligence. Type a research question, select your region and sector, and click ▶ Scan Market. Your first AI-powered report appears in seconds.
4
Chain to the Next Module
At the bottom of any output, find the Chain → buttons. Click one to pre-load that output as context in the next module and navigate there automatically.
5
Use the Co-Pilot
Click APEX Co-Pilot in the sidebar. Describe your business goal in plain language. Co-Pilot recommends the exact module chain, explains what data transfers at each step, and can execute the entire chain autonomously.
⚠ Privacy Note: ApexIQ never transmits your data anywhere except directly to the AI engine you configure. No telemetry, no analytics, no third-party services. Open-Meteo (weather) and OpenStreetMap (POI) use public free APIs with city-name-only queries.
AI-Powered Manual
Every other section of this manual is generated on demand by your configured AI engine — personalised to your session context, cached for the session so the API is called only once per section. Navigate to any section to generate it now.

🌎 Live Intelligence Feeds

✚ Custom RSS Feeds
Add any public RSS URL — proxied via rss2json.
Feeds are cached for 30 minutes to avoid rate limits. Signals are automatically injected into Market Intelligence, Competitive Intel, Risk, and SWARM modules. Click any signal card on the Bridge to preview the article.
Auto-save active for: Country Intelligence, ArchetypeOS, SynthoSurvey.

☁ Google Drive Sync — Intelligence Vault
Saves every module output, CI profiles, ARTEMIS calibration history, SynthoSurvey runs, AESM panel data, SWARM war-game results, prompt engineering data, engine performance telemetry, and cross-session memory — structured for future autonomous retrieval.
Google OAuth Client ID
1. console.cloud.google.com → APIs & Services → Credentials
2. Create OAuth 2.0 Client ID → type: Web application
3. Add this page's URL to Authorised JavaScript origins
4. Enable Google Drive API in API library
5. Paste Client ID above → Connect

Files
Vault Contents
● module-outputs/ — full AI output per module run with inputs & metadata
● ci-profiles/ — full CI JSON + enrichment per country
● artemis-calibrations/ — prediction vs actual log for model tuning
● synthosurvey-runs/ — SSR scores, agent outputs, MPI, verdict per product/market
● aesm-panels/ — full persona panel results, PMF curves, KS statistics
● swarm-sessions/ — war-game briefs, sim results, stress-test outputs
● prompt-kb/ — prompt versions, engine used, token cost, output quality signals
● engine-telemetry/ — latency, success rate, token efficiency per engine
● session-memory/ — cross-session context, chaining patterns, co-pilot objectives
● master-index.json — queryable index of all vault files for autonomous retrieval

ApexIQ v10 — Autonomous Compounding Intelligence Platform
Version History
v10.0.0 — July 2026 · Strategic Compounding Release
ARTEMIS Write-Back Engine: recalibration directives extracted from every delta analysis and stored to localStorage delta store; modules read on init and inject as prompt priors — closes the autonomous learning loop · Vault RAG Co-Pilot Mode: type "vault: [query]" in Co-Pilot to query master-index.json from Drive, retrieve matching session files, and synthesize cross-session meta-analysis · DevAgent Gap Detection: session-start hook reads last 30 AXM runs, identifies analytical gaps, surfaces proactive DevAgent CTA in notifications (runs once per 24h; manual trigger available in Autonomous panel) · V6.1 / ArchetypeOS Chain Enforcer: Co-Pilot injects validation advisory for Pricing, Venture, Marketing, and Finance modules when V6.1 or ArchetypeOS have not been run this session · RSS Auto-Inject: live signals now flow through buildCtxString automatically on every module run without manual "Inject All" click
v19.0.0 — July 2026
Settings rebuilt as 5-page tabbed UI · Token budget display per engine with auto-switch · RSS live feed status indicator · SanDisk/local file export & import · Platform preference toggles (RSS, chain links, notifications) · CI enrichment: button persists on token exhaustion, resume partial enrichment · CI enrichment results chained to all modules · GDPAM PESTLE module built-in · SynthoSurvey Target Event feeds from CI enrichment events · ArchetypeOS archetype auto-determination · Persona Interrelation & Concentration matrix · ARCHETYPEOS INTELLIGENCE REPORT with φ-Dashboard · Persona Match by product/service · Reachable Market population cap fix · Stop-fetch button on all data-fetching modules · Abbreviations glossary
v18.4.0 — 2026
CI Expansion module: 7-step AI enrichment with city profiles, malls, CBDs, workforce, Hofstede, 50+ event calendar, consumer psychology, gender spending, digital behaviour, food culture, fashion/beauty, retail behaviour, social structure, brand hierarchy · Leaflet.js interactive map · CI auto-save & restore enrichment across sessions · ciAddCards / ciAddAISrc / ciAddCSS injection pattern
v18.0.0 — 2026
21 modules · 11 AI engines · ArchetypeOS v3 with ACCS/SFALTV/AMSC/ARAM φ-tools · SynthoSurvey Enterprise 6-agent council · SWARM war-gaming 14-agent · CAVO hallucination firewall · ARTEMIS prediction calibration · MediaPipe AEE · GitHub storage · Google Drive sync · EN/AR bilingual RTL · Module chaining system · φ-Engine Fibonacci/Golden Ratio calibration
Abbreviations Glossary
ACCS — Archetype-Cultural Compatibility Score
AESM — Autonomous Emotion & Sentiment Module
AMSC — Archetype Market Share Ceiling
ARAM — Archetype Revenue Attribution Model
ARTEMIS — Adaptive Real-Time Module Intelligence System
AVC — Average Variable Cost
BI — Business Intelligence
CAC — Customer Acquisition Cost
CAGO — Compound Annual Growth Objective
CAGR — Compound Annual Growth Rate
CAVO — Chief Audit & Validation Officer (SWARM agent)
CBD — Central Business District
CI — Country Intelligence (module)
DCF — Discounted Cash Flow

GLA — Gross Leasable Area
GTM — Go-to-Market
ICP — Ideal Customer Profile

KRI — Key Risk Indicator
KS — Kolmogorov-Smirnov (distribution similarity test)
LTV — Lifetime Value

MPI — Mean Purchase Intent
OSM — OpenStreetMap
PESTLE — Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, Environmental
PMF — Probability Mass Function
POI — Point of Interest
PPP — Purchasing Power Parity
ROAS — Return on Ad Spend
RTL — Right-to-Left (text direction)
SAM — Serviceable Addressable Market
SFALTV — Shadow-Friction Adjusted Lifetime Value
SOM — Serviceable Obtainable Market
SSR — Semantic Similarity Rating (PyMC Labs / Colgate-Palmolive)
SWARM — Strategic War-gaming & Risk Management (14-agent)
SWOT — Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats
TAM — Total Addressable Market
WDCE — Weather-Driven Consumer & Demand Engine
φ — Phi — Golden Ratio (1.618…), Fibonacci engine calibration
